title: Classes & Objects
description: Build templates for custom data types.

slides.title: Creating a Class 📝
slides.content:
To create a class, use the `class` keyword. 

Like structs, properties inside a class belong to it. However, you must use an **access specifier** like `public:` to allow users to interact with those properties from outside the class.

slides.title: Defining the Blueprint
Instantiating 🏭
slides.content: Now that we have a class template defined globally, we can spawn (instantiate) as many `Car` objects as we want down inside our active functions like `main()`!

slides.title: Creating an Object
slides.content: Notice how it looks exactly like the struct example from earlier.

quizData.question: Like structs, class definitions MUST end with a semicolon `};`
quizData.explanation: Yes, block blueprints require a closing semicolon to compile.
